name: spec
description: 仕様駆動開発（SDD）の**上流工程**を担当するSkill。
capabilities: Transform natural language requirements into structured EPIC/Story/Subtask specifications
Generate unambiguous acceptance criteria using EARS notation
Analyze existing codebase and propose technical designs with Mermaid diagrams
Manage workflow with three phases: Requirements, Design, and Task Breakdown
Create file templates with test case scaffolding
Integrate with branch and PR workflows automatically
author: Clionegohan
securitySummary: This skill is pure markdown documentation defining a specification-driven development workflow. No executable code, no file system access, no network capabilities. The 49 static findings are false positives from keyword pattern matching without contextual understanding. Terms like 'Design' (flagged as 'MD5'), 'branch' (flagged as 'hash'), and 'system' (flagged as reconnaissance) are legitimate workflow terminology misidentified by the scanner.
valueStatement: Writing clear software specifications from requirements is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill transforms natural language requirements into structured EPIC/Story/Subtask specifications using EARS notation through an interactive three-phase workflow.
limitations: Does not execute code or modify existing files automatically
Requires user confirmation at each phase transition
Does not validate requirements against external systems

useCases.title: Define feature requirements
useCases.description: Convert vague feature ideas into precise, testable specifications that developers can implement directly

useCases.title: Structure technical designs
useCases.description: Generate comprehensive technical specifications including data flow diagrams and interface definitions

useCases.title: Create backlog items
useCases.description: Transform user requests into well-structured backlog items with clear acceptance criteria and task breakdowns

promptTemplates.title: New feature spec
promptTemplates.prompt: ユーザー認証機能のspecを作成して。OAuth対応と多要素認証を含めて

promptTemplates.title: Refinement request
promptTemplates.prompt: 既存の仕様にセキュリティ要件を追加して。認証フローとデータ暗号化を含めて

promptTemplates.title: API specification
promptTemplates.prompt: REST APIのspecを作成して。エンドポイント定義とリクエスト/レスポンス形式を含めて

promptTemplates.title: Full workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: 新機能のspecを最初から作成して。要件定義からタスク分割まで一貫して依頼する

bestPractices: Start with a clear, concise feature description including key requirements
Confirm each phase before proceeding to ensure alignment with your vision
Review generated acceptance criteria for completeness before implementation
antiPatterns: Providing overly vague requirements leads to unclear specifications
Skipping phase confirmations causes misalignment with user intent
Requesting implementation code in the specification phase

faq.question: What platforms support this skill?
faq.answer: Compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Works across all platforms where skills are supported.

faq.question: How detailed should my initial request be?
faq.answer: Include the feature name, key requirements, and any specific constraints. More detail produces better specifications.

faq.question: Can I modify specifications after generation?
faq.answer: Yes. Use '修正して' command at any confirmation point, or manually edit the generated markdown files.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: This skill only processes text locally. No data is sent to external servers. Generated files remain in your repository.

faq.question: Why does the skill ask for confirmation at each phase?
faq.answer: User confirmation ensures the specification aligns with your intent before proceeding. This prevents wasted effort.

faq.question: How is this different from regular prompt engineering?
faq.answer: This skill enforces a structured SDD methodology with three distinct phases, EARS notation, and automatic branch/PR integration.
